01 September 2025 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Live Training Event Enhances Readiness for U.S., ROK Soldiers Army News 01 Sep 2025 -- U.S. and Republic of Korea Soldiers participated in a combined training exercise at the Wall Fitness Center here on August 27, 2025.

  • Deputy Secretary General affirms strong NATO support for Ukraine in Bled, Slovenia NATO 01 Sep 2025 -- On Monday (1 September 2025), NATO Deputy Secretary General Radmila Shekerinska participated in the Bled Strategic Forum, Slovenia. On the panel "Ukraine: Long Road to Peace," she affirmed NATO's continued support for Ukraine, as Allies work to ensure Ukraine can defend itself today, and deter future aggression.

  • President of Ukraine and Prime Minister of Portugal Discussed Security Guarantees and Joint Drone Production President of Ukraine 01 Sep 2025
  • Investments in Drone Production and Continued Defense Support: Meeting with Norwegian Delegation Took Place at the Office of the President President of Ukraine 01 Sep 2025
  • Volodymyr Zelenskyy and Micheál Martin Discussed Security Guarantees and Ireland's Readiness to Contribute President of Ukraine 01 Sep 2025
  • Europe 'Moving Forward' On Plans For Ukraine Peace Force With US Backing, Von Der Leyen Says RFE/RL 01 Sep 2025 -- Kyiv's European allies are working on "pretty precise plans" and a "clear road map" for a potential deployment of troops to Ukraine should a peace deal be struck between Kyiv and Moscow, European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen said.
  • Denys Shmyhal: The network of military lyceums in Ukraine will be expanded Ukraine MOD 01 Sep 2025
  • At an extraordinary meeting of NATO-Ukraine Council Allies confirmed their support for Ukraine in countering Russian terror Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Ukraine 01 Sep 2025 -- On 1 September 2025, an extraordinary meeting of NATO-Ukraine Council in the format of the Political Affairs Committee was held at NATO headquarters in Brussels, convened at the request of the Ukrainian side following the recent massive Russian attacks.
  • In August, units of the Ministry of Defence cleared nearly 6,000 hectares of de-occupied territory Ukraine MOD 01 Sep 2025

  • Strengthening disaster relief cooperation in Tonga Australia DOD 01 Sep 2025 -- On July 30, a magnitude-8.8 earthquake struck off the eastern coast of the Kamchatka Peninsula in Russia, resulting in tsunami alerts being issued across the Pacific.
  • Army active in the fuel security transition Australia DOD 01 Sep 2025 -- The first 15,000-litre renewable diesel delivery to Army's School of Armour (SOARM) Puckapunyal is already powering the fleet.

  • INDIAN ARMY CONTINGENT DEPARTS FOR INDIA - USA JOINT MILITARY EXERCISE YUDH ABHYAS 2025 India PIB 01 Sep 2025 -- An Indian Army contingent has departed for Fort Wainwright, Alaska, USA, to participate in the 21st edition of the India-USA Joint Military Exercise Yudh Abhyas 2025, scheduled from 1st to 14th September 2025
  • INDIAN NAVAL SHIPS TAMAL AND SURAT DEPART JEDDAH, SAUDI ARABIA India PIB 01 Sep 2025 -- Indian Naval warships INS Tamal and INS Surat concluded their port call at Jeddah, Saudi Arabia on 30 Aug 2025. The ships participated in a Passage Exercise with RSNF Corvette HMS Jazan and thereafter continued with their planned deployment.

  • Lieutenant General Thiab Saqer Abdulla Al Nuaimi, Chief of Staff, Bahrain Defence Force, called on Air Chief Marshal Zaheer Ahmed Baber Sidhu, Chief of the Air Staff, Pakistan Air Force during his visit to Air Headquarters, Islamabad. ISPR Pakistan 01 Sep 2025
  • On 1 Sep 25, at around 1000 hours, an MI-17 helicopter crash landed near Hudor village approximately 12 kilometers away from Thakdas Cantonment. ISPR Pakistan 01 Sep 2025

  • Cyber & Specialist Operations Command - Established to tackle the threats of today and tomorrow UK MOD 01 Sep 2025 -- The Ministry of Defence has reshaped the Cyber & Specialist Operations Command (CSOC) to enhance the UK's ability to help keep the United Kingdom secure at home and strong abroad.

  • Opening of the Autonomous Systems Competence Center in Riga Latvia MOD 01 Sep 2025 -- On Wednesday, September 3, A6 Logistics Park, Riga Minister of Defence of Latvia Andris Sprūds will inaugurate the Autonomous Systems Competence Center. The new center will serve as a national platform for the development and integration of drone technologies within the Latvian National Armed Forces.

  • Keynote speech by President António Costa at the opening of the Bled Strategic Forum EC/CEU 01 Sep 2025

Defense Industry

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

    Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • PM Netanyahu meets with heads of local authorities in the North Israel - Prime Minister 01 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Netanyahu in a meeting with heads of local authorities in the North: "They refer to it as "Tuscany", but this came before Tuscany. It's the Land of the Bible, the land of our heritage. We are going to develop the Galilee in leaps and bounds".

  • Israel says Hamas armed wing spokesman killed in Gaza strike People's Daily 01 Sep 2025 -- Israeli Defense Minister Israel Katz said on Sunday that Abu Obeida, the spokesman of Hamas's armed wing, had been killed in a strike in the Gaza Strip.

  • 'Gaza not for sale': Hamas condemns US plan for takeover of Gaza Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- The Palestinian resistance movement Hamas has condemned a plan reportedly under consideration by US President Donald Trump that will place the Gaza Strip under American control and forcibly relocate its population.

  • Hezbollah member killed in Israeli airstrike on S. Lebanon: sources People's Daily 01 Sep 2025 -- A Hezbollah member was killed Sunday in an Israeli airstrike targeting southern Lebanon, according to Lebanese sources.

  • US blocks visa access for Palestinians ahead of statehood recognition Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- American officials say Washington has halted the approval of visas for Palestinian passport holders, a move coming just as international momentum builds toward recognizing a Palestinian state.

  • Iran's military chief condemns Israel's assassination of Yemeni officials IRNA 01 Sep 2025 -- Chairman of the Chief of Staff of the Iranian Armed Forces Major General Abdolrahim Mousavi has condemned Israel's assassination of several top Yemeni officials, saying that "this crime" reflects a profound fear of the expanding axis of resistance and the unity of regional nations in defending the Palestinian cause and countering Israeli occupation.
  • Pezeshkian calls for stronger UN action against Israel's crimes in West Asia IRNA 01 Sep 2025 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian has called on the United Nations to take practical measures to put an end to Israel's crimes and warmongering in the West Asia region.
  • Israel's 'cowardly' assassination of Yemeni officials is a heinous crime: Araghchi IRNA 01 Sep 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has condemned Israel's "cowardly" assassination of several top Yemeni officials, including Prime Minister Ahmed Ghaleb al-Rahawi, describing it as a heinous and unprecedented crime against the free nation of Yemen.
  • Pezeshkian: UN must take 'practical' action to end Israel's atrocities Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian says international organizations, particularly the United Nations, must take "practical and more serious" actions to put an end to the Israeli regime's crimes and warmongering in the region.
  • UN inaction on Israeli crimes poses 'unprecedented threat' to global peace: Iran Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- The Iranian foreign minister has censured the United Nations for its inaction on Israel's crimes in the West Asian region, saying the situation poses an "unprecedented threat" to international security and an "existential danger" to humanity.

  • "There can be no excuse for failing to stop the atrocities in Gaza" Presidency of The Republic of Turkey 01 Sep 2025 -- Addressing the "SCO Plus" session of the 25th SCO summit, President Erdoğan said: "There can be no excuse for failing, for 23 months, to stop the atrocities in Gaza in which babies, children and the elderly are being starved to death. I believe that it is our collective responsibility to turn the United Nations into a platform that represents global justice in the face of the oppression the Palestinian people, particularly those in Gaza, have been subjected to for years."
  • Foreign Secretary statement on the Middle East UK FCDO 01 Sep 2025 -- Foreign Secretary statement on the Middle East delivered to Parliament on 1 September 2025.

  • Yemeni forces target Israeli oil tanker in Red Sea with ballistic missile Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- Yemen's Armed Forces have carried out a missile strike on an Israeli oil tanker in the Red Sea for breaching a ban on ships bound for ports in the Israeli-occupied territories imposed in solidarity with Gaza.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Statement attributable to the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General - on Afghanistan UN Secretary-General 01 Sep 2025
  • Thousands of children at risk after devastating earthquake hits eastern Afghanistan UNICEF 01 Sep 2025 -- Statement by UNICEF Afghanistan Representative, Dr. Tajudeen Oyewale, on the earthquake in Nangarhar and Kunar provinces
  • Aid effort underway after Afghanistan quake 'wipes out' villages UN News 01 Sep 2025 -- After a magnitude six earthquake struck remote areas of eastern Afghanistan overnight reportedly killing at least 800 people and wiping out villages, UN chief António Guterres on Monday pledged to "spare no effort" in helping those affected.

  • Guterres condemns detention of more UN staff in Yemen UN News 01 Sep 2025 -- UN Secretary-General António Guterres has strongly condemned the arbitrary detention of at least 11 staff members in Yemen on Sunday by the de facto Houthi authorities in areas under their control.

  • OSCE Ministerial Council decision marks new step towards sustainable peace in the South Caucasus OSCE 01 Sep 2025 -- The OSCE Chairperson-in-Office, Foreign Minister of Finland Elina Valtonen, and OSCE Secretary General Feridun H. Sinirlioğlu welcome the adoption of a Ministerial Council decision closing the Minsk process and related structures following a Joint Appeal from Armenia and Azerbaijan.
  • Iran opposes presence of external forces in Caucasus region, says Pezeshkian IRNA 01 Sep 2025 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian says Iran supports the recently signed peace deal between Armenia and Azerbaijan, but at the same time, opposes the presence of extra-regional forces in the Caucasus region.

News Reports

  • Russia Suspected Of Jamming GPS As EU Chief's Plane Landed In Bulgaria RFE/RL 01 Sep 2025 -- The GPS navigation system of European Commission President Ursula von der Leyen's was jammed as it prepared to land over the weekend, Bulgarian officials said, sparking accusations of a Russian interference operation.

  • Secretary-General's remarks to the Shahghai Cooperation Organization [as prepared] UN Secretary-General 01 Sep 2025
  • Shanghai Cooperation Organization summit: Guterres calls for strengthening multilateralism UN News 01 Sep 2025 -- UN Secretary-General António Guterres called for strengthening multilateralism - and appealed for peace in Gaza, Ukraine and beyond - in remarks to the Shanghai Cooperation Organization summit in Tianjin, China, on Monday.

  • SCO Heads of State Council Meeting President of Russia 01 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin took part in the SCO Heads of State Council Meeting.
  • SCO Plus meeting President of Russia 01 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin took part in the SCO Plus meeting.
  • Putin: SCO 'locomotive of multipolarity,' catalyst for global development Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin has described the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) as a "powerful locomotive of multipolarity" and a key driver of global development.
  • Russia Backs China's Initiative on Global Governance: Putin at SCO Sputnik 01 Sep 2025 -- Russia supports the global governance initiative put forward by Chinese President Xi Jinping and is interested in moving to a concrete discussion of the proposals, Russian President Vladimir Putin said.
  • Putin Blames West For Ukraine War As Xi Touts Diplomatic Clout At SCO Summit RFE/RL 01 Sep 2025 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin sent a defiant message against the West over his invasion of Ukraine after standing shoulder-to-shoulder with Chinese leader Xi Jinping as the two leaders showcased their close ties at the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) summit in China.
  • Meeting with Prime Minister of India Narendra Modi President of Russia 01 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin and Prime Minister of the Republic of India Narendra Modi met on the sidelines of the SCO summit.
  • Putin, Modi Talking Face-to-Face Before Talks With Participation of Delegations - Kremlin Sputnik 01 Sep 2025 -- Russian President Vladimir Putin and Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi are talking face-to-face before bilateral talks with the participation of delegations, Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov said on Monday.
  • Russia, India Sign Several Major Aviation Contracts in 2025 Sputnik 01 Sep 2025 -- Russia and India signed several major contracts related to aviation in 2025, Russian Federal Service for Military-Technical Cooperation chief Dmitry Shugaev said on Monday.
  • Meeting with President of Iran Masoud Pezeshkian President of Russia 01 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin held talks with President of the Islamic Republic of Iran Masoud Pezeshkian.
  • Meeting with President of Turkiye Recep Tayyip Erdogan President of Russia 01 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin met with the President of the Republic of Turkiye, Recep Tayyip Erdogan on the sidelines of the SCO summit.
  • Meeting with President of Tajikistan Emomali Rahmon President of Russia 01 Sep 2025 -- Vladimir Putin and President of Tajikistan Emomali Rahmon met on the sidelines of the SCO summit.
  • Meeting with Prime Minister of Vietnam Pham Minh Chinh President of Russia 01 Sep 2025 -- Following the SCO Plus meeting, Vladimir Putin met with Prime Minister of the Socialist Republic of Vietnam Pham Minh Chinh.
  • Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's answer to a question from the Moscow.Kremlin.Putin programme, Tianjin, China, September 1, 2025 Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 01 Sep 2025
  • Russia, China to Expand Nuclear Energy Cooperation in Near Future - Russia's Rosatom CEO Sputnik 01 Sep 2025 -- In the near future, Russia and China will expand bilateral cooperation in the field of nuclear energy, as well as non-energy applications of peaceful nuclear technologies, including quantum ones, Russian state nuclear corporation Rosatom CEO Alexey Likhachev said on Monday.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on September 1, 2025 PRC MofA 01 Sep 2025
  • President Xi Jinping Chairs the 25th Meeting of the Council of Heads of State of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization and Delivers an Important Statement PRC MofA 01 Sep 2025 -- On the morning of September 1, 2025, the 25th Meeting of the Council of Heads of State of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) was held at the Tianjin Meijiang Convention and Exhibition Center. President Xi Jinping presided over the meeting and delivered an important statement titled "Staying True to SCO Founding Mission and Ushering in a Better Future."
  • Pooling the Strength of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization to Improve Global Governance PRC MofA 01 Sep 2025 -- Statement by H.E. Xi Jinping, President of the People's Republic of China At the "Shanghai Cooperation Organization Plus" Meeting
  • Staying True to SCO Founding Mission And Ushering in a Better Future PRC MofA 01 Sep 2025 -- Statement by H.E. Xi Jinping, President of the People's Republic of China At the 25th Meeting of the Council of Heads of State Of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization
  • Wang Yi and SCO Secretary-General Jointly Meet the Press PRC MofA 01 Sep 2025 -- On September 1, 2025, upon the conclusion of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) Tianjin Summit, Member of the Political Bureau of the CPC Central Committee and Foreign Minister Wang Yi, together with SCO Secretary-General Nurlan Yermekbayev, met Chinese and foreign press at the Media Center of the Summit and answered questions.
  • All SCO Leaders Back Xi Jinping's Global Governance Initiative Sputnik 01 Sep 2025 -- On Monday, Chinese President Xi Jinping proposed the Global Governance Initiative (GGI), urging nations to join forces in building a fairer and more balanced system of global governance.
  • SCO Countries Must Resist Bloc Confrontation - Xi Jinping Sputnik 01 Sep 2025 -- The countries of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) must resist bloc confrontation and bullying, and defend the world order with the central role of the UN, Chinese President Xi Jinping said on Monday at the 25th meeting of the Council of SCO Heads of State.
  • China's Xi slams 'bullying' behavior in world affairs, urges cooperation to strengthen SCO Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- Chinese President Xi Jinping has criticized "bullying behavior" in international affairs, pledging to strengthen multilateral ties as he opened the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) summit in Tianjin.
  • Chinese FM responds to Indonesian President's cancellation of visit to China for the SCO Summit and other events Global Times 01 Sep 2025 -- According to reports, the escalating large-scale demonstrations in Jakarta and other parts of Indonesia have caused casualties; Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto's cancelled his trip to China for the SCO Summit and other events. What is China's comment on the situation?

  • Court orders detention of 3 over alleged TSMC trade secret theft CNA 01 Sep 2025 -- The Intellectual Property and Commercial Court (IPCC) has ordered one former employee and two current employees of Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) be detained and held incommunicado for allegedly stealing sensitive trade secrets involving the company's advanced 2 nanometer process.
  • St. Lucia deputy PM, ministers visit Taiwan CNA 01 Sep 2025 -- Saint Lucian Deputy Prime Minister Ernest Hilaire has arrived in Taiwan for a five-day visit to meet with President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) and enhance bilateral exchanges, the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MOFA) said Monday.
  • Government, parties outline priorities as new lawmaking session begins CNA 01 Sep 2025 -- The ruling Democratic Progressive Party (DPP), the main opposition Kuomintang (KMT) and the smaller Taiwan People's Party (TPP) each commented on their respective legislative priorities Monday as the Legislative Yuan began a new session.
  • Presidential Office announces 4 National Security Council appointments CNA 01 Sep 2025 -- Two deputy secretary-generals in Taiwan's National Security Council (NSC) have been named advisors, while a Presidential Office spokesperson and a Taipei City councilor will fill their vacancies, the Presidential Office said Monday.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 01 Sep 2025

  • Iran Defiant As 'Snapback' Sanctions Loom But May Not Have Many Options RFE/RL 01 Sep 2025 -- Iranian Vice President Mohammad Reza Aref has vowed that Tehran will not "bow to pressure" despite the impending triggering of a UN mechanism that would restore sanctions over its alleged "grave noncompliance" of the 2015 nuclear deal.
  • SCO declaration 'strongly' condemns US-Israeli aggression against Iran Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- The Shanghai Cooperation Organization member states, in a joint statement on Monday, "strongly" condemned the US-Israeli aggression against Iran.
  • Iran, China, Russia sign joint letter reaffirming illegality of E3's snapback move IRNA 01 Sep 2025 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says Iran along with Russia and China have reaffirmed that the move by E3 countries, namely Britain, France, and Germany, to trigger the "snapback mechanism" against the Islamic Republic is void and without any legal basis.
  • China, Russia join Iran in rejecting E3's 'legally baseless' snapback move: Araghchi Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi says he, together with his Russian and Chinese counterparts, has sent a letter to the United Nations rejecting as "legally baseless" and "politically destructive" a recent move by Britain, France, and Germany to reinstate international sanctions against Iran.
  • Iran open to diplomatic solution on nuclear program: President Pezeshkian IRNA 01 Sep 2025 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian says the Islamic Republic of Iran is and has always been ready to find a diplomatic solution to peacefully resolve its nuclear issue, emphasizing that the failure of the June attacks by the US and Israel on Iran's nuclear sites demonstrated the ineffectiveness of military action.
  • Iran president advises US, Europe to end confrontation, return to diplomacy Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian of Iran says the United States and Europe had better stop approaches based on confrontation and engage in diplomacy to find a "balanced and fair" solution to Tehran's nuclear issue.
  • SCO member states reject biased interpretation of UNSC Resolution 2231: Top diplomat IRNA 01 Sep 2025 -- Deputy Foreign Minister for Legal and International Affairs, Kazem Gharibabadi, says that leaders of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) have voiced opposition to Europe's selective interpretation of United Nations Security Council (UNSC) Resolution 2231.
  • Iran says SCO leaders oppose Europe's 'selective' interpretation of Resolution 2231 Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- The Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO), in its final summit declaration, is set to oppose the European troika's "selective" interpretation of Resolution 2231, a senior Iranian official says.
  • Iran president urges SCO to take concrete steps toward peace, economic cooperation Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian says the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) should take practical and clear steps to promote global peace and economic cooperation.
  • Iran, Russia presidents highlight strengthening ties via long-term pact IRNA 01 Sep 2025 -- President Masoud Pezeshkian and his Russian counterpart Vladimir Putin have emphasized the importance of implementing the Treaty on the Comprehensive Strategic Partnership between Iran and Russia.
  • Iran, Russia presidents call for full implementation of strategic partnership treaty Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian and Russian President Vladimir Putin met on Monday on the sidelines of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) summit in Tianjin, China, and agreed to step up efforts to fully implement a comprehensive strategic partnership treaty between the two allies.
  • Iran starts moving giant platform to South Pars gas field Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- Iran has started the transport of a huge rig jacket from a yard on the coast of the Persian Gulf to one of the largest sections of its South Pars gas field.
  • Chief banker denies Iran oil revenues fell in August Press TV 01 Sep 2025 -- The Governor of the Central Bank of Iran (CBI) has denied speculations that the country's oil export revenues fell in the calendar month to late August because of a war in June with the Israeli regime that reportedly affected overseas supplies in later months.

  • English Translation of Prime Minister's statement during the 25th SCO Summit India PIB 01 Sep 2025
  • Prime Minister participates in the 25th SCO Summit in Tianjin, China (September 01, 2025) India MEA 01 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi participated in the 25th Meeting of the Council of Heads of State of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO), held in Tianjin, China, from 31 August to 1 September 2025.
  • Modi thanks Chinese leader, government and people for successful SCO Summit organization Global Times 01 Sep 2025 -- Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi said on social media platform X on Monday that his visit to China is productive, thanking the Chinese president, Chinese government and people for the successful organization of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) Summit 2025.
  • Prime Minister meets President of the Russian Federation (September 01, 2025) India MEA 01 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Shri Narendra Modi met today with the President of the Russian Federation, H.E Mr. Vladimir Putin on the sidelines of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization Summit in Tianjin, China.
  • Prime Minister's Opening statement during his meeting with the President of Russia India PIB 01 Sep 2025 -- I am very pleased to meet you, and I feel that meeting you is always an occasion to remember

  • Focus on India: Foreign Minister Wadephul visits strategic partner in the Indo-Pacific Germany Federal Foreign Office 01 Sep 2025 -- Foreign Minister Wadephul's trip to Bangalore and New Delhi will centre on the strategic partnership, which Germany and India intend to intensify through even closer cooperation across the entire spectrum. Dialogue on business, technology, space and security will also be the focus of attention.
  • Statement by Foreign Minister Wadephul prior to his departure for India Germany Federal Foreign Office 01 Sep 2025

  • Participation of the Prime Minister of Pakistan in the SCO Council of Heads of State (CHS) and SCO CHS Plus Meetings in Tianjin, China Pakistan Ministry of Foreign Affairs 01 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif led Pakistan delegation to the Summit meetings of SCO Council of Heads of State (CHS) and SCO CHS Plus presided by President Xi Jinping of China in Tianjin, China on 31 August - 01 September 2025.
  • The Prime Minister of Pakistan meets the President of Iran Pakistan - Prime Miinister's Office 01 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif held a meeting with the President of the Islamic Republic of Iran, Masoud Pezeshkian, on the sidelines of the Council of Heads of Member States (CHM) of the Shanghai Cooperation Organization (SCO) in Tianjin, China.
  • Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif in an address to the SCO (Plus), held in Tianjin, China, Pakistan - Prime Miinister's Office 01 Sep 2025
  • Meeting between the President of Azerbaijan and and the Prime Minister of Pakistan Pakistan - Prime Miinister's Office 01 Sep 2025 -- Prime Minister Muhammad Shehbaz Sharif held a meeting with H.E. Ilham Aliyev, President of the Republic of Azerbaijan, on the sidelines of the SCO Council of Heads of State (CHS) being convened in Tianjin, China from 31 August to 01 September 2025.

  • Second Japan-Gulf Cooperation Council (GCC) Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 Sep 2025
  • Meeting between Foreign Minister IWAYA and H.E. Mr. Sultan bin Saad Al-Muraikhi, State Minister for Foreign Affairs of the State of Qatar Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 Sep 2025
  • Courtesy Call on Prime Minister Ahmed Abdullah of Kuwait by Foreign Minister Iwaya Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 Sep 2025
  • Courtesy Call on Crown Prince Sabah Khalid of Kuwait by Foreign Minister Iwaya Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 Sep 2025
  • Meeting between Prime Minister ISHIBA Shigeru and Prime Minister Dorin Recean of the Republic of Moldova Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 Sep 2025
  • Japan-Kuwait Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 Sep 2025
  • Japan-Bahrain Foreign Ministers' Conversation Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 Sep 2025
  • Japan-Oman Foreign Ministers' Meeting Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 01 Sep 2025

  • Statement by President von der Leyen with Romanian President Dan European Commission 01 Sep 2025

  • State secretary Varteressian's remarks on the new High North - Arctic policy Government of Norway 01 Sep 2025

  • Statement by President von der Leyen with Lithuanian President Nausėda European Commission 01 Sep 2025
  • Gitanas Nausėda met with the President of the European Commission at the Lithuania-Belarus border: Baltic Defense Line must become an EU priority President of the Republic of Lithuania 01 Sep 2025 -- On Monday, President Gitanas Nausėda met with the President of the European Commission, Ursula von der Leyen, at the Lithuania-Belarus border to review the border security situation.

  • Indonesia protests: Call for restraint and dialogue OHCHR 01 Sep 2025

  • Burundi: UN experts alarmed by increase in serious human rights violations during election period OHCHR 01 Sep 2025 -- UN experts* today expressed deep concern about the increase in serious human rights violations in Burundi, that has included attacks against civil society and political opponents and election violence.

  • Back To School In Serbia? Universities Seek To Break Student Boycott RFE/RL 01 Sep 2025 -- Serbian universities are hoping to reopen their doors in September following months of student boycotts, but Jelena Stankovic is adamant: "I will not return to my studies."
